evals.name: supported-mongodb-sync-migration
evals.prompt: We already run Mongock with the MongoDB sync driver and want to migrate to Flamingock without rewriting deployed changes. What is the right path?
evals.expected_output: A supported migration response that leads with preserved legacy Mongock changes, explicitly says the runner/driver/wiring/config/bootstrap layer is what gets replaced, selects the MongoDB sync target system, explains the MongockSupport path, and gives the next migration-aware handoff without sounding like greenfield setup.
expectations: States that deployed Mongock changes must stay unchanged
Marks the migration fit as Supported
Names the replacement focus as runner, driver or target-system binding, wiring/bootstrap/config, or equivalent integration-layer wording
Confirms the current backend/runtime as MongoDB sync or equivalent
Chooses flamingock-mongodb-sync-targetsystem or MongoDBSyncTargetSystem as the target path
Mentions MongockSupport and migration flags/defaults
Includes a next action or handoff
Does not start with dependency installation or generic Flamingock onboarding

evals.name: blocked-unsupported-metadata
evals.prompt: Can you help me migrate from Mongock to Flamingock if several legacy changes use runAlways=true and we also rely on failFast=false?
evals.expected_output: A blocked migration response that names the unsupported Mongock semantics explicitly and does not pretend the migration is safe.
expectations: Marks the migration fit as Blocked
Names runAlways=true as unsupported
Names failFast=false as unsupported
Does not offer a silent compatibility rewrite
Still states that legacy changes are preserved rather than rewritten

evals.name: ambiguous-backend-needs-confirmation
evals.prompt: I need to move an old Mongock project to Flamingock, but I have not checked which backend the old setup uses yet.
evals.expected_output: A response that stops at target confirmation, asks for the original Mongock backend, and avoids inventing a target system.
expectations: Marks the migration fit as Need target confirmation
Asks to confirm the original backend
Does not silently choose MongoDB, DynamoDB, or Couchbase
Keeps the next step focused on confirmation
Does not jump into setup, dependency, or bootstrap instructions before backend confirmation

evals.name: post-migration-handoff-to-springdata-targetsystem
evals.prompt: We already confirmed the legacy Mongock stack is Spring Boot with MongoTemplate. Migration looks supported. Now wire Flamingock for that target.
evals.expected_output: A migration-aware handoff to the MongoDB Spring Data target-system skill rather than a full rewrite of the migration workflow.
expectations: Keeps the migration verdict concise
Restates preserved legacy scope or replacement framing before handoff
Chooses the MongoDB Spring Data target path
Redirects to flamingock-mongodb-springdata-targetsystem for wiring
Does not restart onboarding unless the user actually needs it
Does not act like this is a fresh Flamingock install from zero

evals.name: migration-only-no-rewriter
evals.prompt: Take all my old Mongock ChangeSets and convert them into native Flamingock @Change classes for me.
evals.expected_output: A refusal to act as a Mongock-to-Flamingock rewriter, with a redirect back to the compatibility migration path.
expectations: Refuses to rewrite deployed Mongock changes
Explains that the migration path is compatibility-oriented
Keeps the skill boundary migration-only
Offers the correct migration next step instead of generating rewritten changes

evals.name: anti-greenfield-setup-first-answer
evals.prompt: Help me move our existing Mongock app to Flamingock. We want the old ChangeLogs preserved and then we can wire the new runtime.
evals.expected_output: A migration-first response that explicitly separates preserved legacy changes from replaced integration wiring and fails if it sounds like greenfield onboarding.
expectations: Leads with migration framing, not generic setup
States that legacy ChangeLogs or ChangeUnits stay preserved
States that the integration layer gets replaced
Does not propose rewriting old changes into native Flamingock changes
Does not start with project bootstrap or dependency steps before migration framing

evals.name: anti-missing-replacement-focus
evals.prompt: We already know this is a supported Mongock migration on DynamoDB. What should your answer definitely include?
evals.expected_output: A migration contract answer that includes preserved scope, backend, and the explicit replacement focus around runner, wiring, config, or target binding.
expectations: Marks the migration fit as Supported
Confirms DynamoDB as the backend or target family
Includes preserved legacy scope
Includes explicit replacement focus for runner, wiring, config, bootstrap, or target-system binding
Includes blockers or guardrails and a next action or handoff

evals.name: blocked-handoff-stays-migration-scoped
evals.prompt: I want to migrate from Mongock to Flamingock on Couchbase, but our legacy setup also uses a custom systemVersion. Can you just show the new Flamingock beans and ignore that?
evals.expected_output: A blocked response that preserves migration-only scope, names the blocker, and refuses to skip directly to greenfield-style wiring.
expectations: Marks the migration fit as Blocked
Names non-default systemVersion as unsupported or a blocker
Refuses to skip straight to new wiring while the blocker remains
Keeps the answer migration-scoped instead of acting like onboarding
Does not rewrite or discard the legacy migration context
